mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of rate-cut periods, or rolling Rank IC < 0.02 over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.0%, labor market weakness signals deteriorating consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 6.0%, or signal accuracy drops below 50% in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, which translates into elevated corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when either series declines, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days during post-recession recovery phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 20-day returns during recovery periods, or the signal reverses direction when tested in late-cycle expansions.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls signal robust economic activity and wage growth, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the following 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Payroll growth consistently coincides with negative 5-day forward returns, or the signal predictive power vanishes when controlling for prior month trends.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 235.0, accelerating inflation triggers expectations of tighter monetary policy and higher discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL readings above 235.0 are followed by positive 60-day returns in >55% of cases, or the threshold fails to separate return distributions significantly.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real GDP and retail sales confirms synchronized demand expansion across macro aggregates, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal loses predictive accuracy when GDP growth decouples from retail sales, or out-of-sample R-squared for the interaction term falls below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in industrial production during late-cycle expansions indicate supply-side bottlenecks and fading momentum, forecasting negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during late-cycle phases are followed by positive 5-day returns in >60% of instances, or the signal performs identically across all cycle phases.
